name: ruby-memoizable
title: ruby-memoizable
description:
memoize method return values
Memoization is an optimization that saves the return value of a method
so it doesn't need to be re-computed every time that method is called.
.
Learn more about its rationale and usage at
/usr/share/ruby-memoizable/README.md
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use ruby-memoizable because memoize method return values.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use ruby-memoizable as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair ruby-memoizable with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ruby-memoizable --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
